revenue-explorer.tagline: Answer revenue questions over the demo orders dataset — no SQL required.
revenue-explorer.description:
**Revenue Explorer** turns plain-English revenue questions into queries over the demo `orders_demo` table:

- Total revenue and average order value over a date range.
- Revenue trend by day, week, or month.
- Top order days and outliers.

Every read runs locally against the bundled synthetic dataset — zero cost, zero risk.

use_cases.title: Revenue over time
use_cases.description: Aggregate revenue across a date range.
use_cases.prompt: What was total revenue per month over the last 120 days?

use_cases.title: Average order value
use_cases.prompt: What is the average order value, and how has it changed?

revenue-explorer.tagline: Summarise revenue and order value over a date range.
revenue-explorer.description: Aggregate the demo orders table into revenue, order count, and average order value, optionally grouped by time period.
revenue-explorer.when_to_use: Use when you have a revenue question over the demo orders dataset and want an answer without writing SQL.

cohort-helper.tagline: Group demo customers into cohorts and track repeat purchases.
cohort-helper.description:
**Cohort Helper** joins `orders_demo` to `customers_demo` to build simple cohorts:

- Customers grouped by first-order month or country.
- Repeat-purchase rates per cohort.
- Cohort comparison over time.

All analysis is local against the bundled synthetic dataset.

use_cases.title: Repeat purchasers
use_cases.description: Find customers with more than one order.
use_cases.prompt: How many customers placed more than one order?

use_cases.title: Cohort by country
use_cases.prompt: Which countries have the highest repeat-purchase rate?

cohort-helper.tagline: Build customer cohorts and measure repeat purchases.
cohort-helper.description: Join the demo orders and customers tables to group customers into cohorts and compute repeat-purchase metrics.
cohort-helper.when_to_use: Use when you want to segment demo customers into cohorts and compare their behaviour.
